compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
THE security-critical divergence. e165 (change 563, commit 2efdae38f) implemented key
validity as an **HLC-epoch** window (ordinal position in the reducer's canonical replay).
The APPROVED epic design requires **git-commit-ancestry** validity (tickets-branch commit
SHAs). HLC ordering is partly author-assigned, so a malicious clone holding a
later-revoked key can write an event with a backdated HLC that sorts BEFORE the revocation
and verifies as valid — defeating the feature's "not attacker-controllable" promise. The
immutable commit DAG closes that hole. (User rejected the delivered design; see d7c0.)

## Why the DAG is stronger
Commit SHAs chain content+parents, so an event's position in history is a tamper-proof
fact — you cannot make a new event an ancestor of an existing revocation commit without
forging SHAs. HLC ordering trusts the author-assigned timestamp component; commit ancestry
trusts only the DAG.

## Architecture decision — pure reducer + git-aware resolver (READ FIRST)
The reducer (`_processors.py`) is **pure-filesystem** today (no git/subprocess) and MUST
stay so (its output is cache-keyed; it runs on plain event-file directories). Commit SHAs
are git metadata not in event JSON, so we do NOT store SHAs in reduced state. Instead:

- **Reducer keyring fold (pure):** records each key's lifecycle by **event position** (the
  `{HLC-ts}-{uuid}` filename prefix the reducer already parses) — record shape
  `{public_key, added_at: <position>, revoked_at: <position|None>}`. Drop `added_epoch` /
  `revoked_epoch` / `keyring_epoch`. `state["keys"]` = keys with `revoked_at is None`.
- **Git-aware resolver (new, in `authorship.py`, the attest layer — NOT the reducer):**
  - `resolve_event_commit(position, ticket_dir, *, repo_root) -> str | None`: maps a
    position prefix to the tickets-branch commit SHA that introduced that event file.
    **TYPE is NOT an input** to the resolver, so it globs the position prefix:
    `git log --diff-filter=A --format=%H -- "<ticket_dir>/<position>-*.json"` run in the
    tracker dir, taking the (single) introducing commit. Returns `None` on any git failure
    or no match (fail-closed).
  - `verify_authorship_at_commit(envelope, identity_id, event_commit, event_position, *,
    repo_root) -> Verdict`: builds the trust root from exactly the keys valid at
    `event_commit`. A key K is valid iff **`merge-base --is-ancestor(K.added_commit,
    event_commit)` AND NOT `merge-base --is-ancestor(K.revoked_commit, event_commit)`**,
    where `K.added_commit`/`K.revoked_commit` come from resolving K's `added_at`/`revoked_at`
    positions. This is where the AC's commit-anchored record
    `{pubkey, added_at_commit, revoked_at_commit}` materializes (resolver output). ANY git
    subprocess failure (`git log` / `merge-base` non-zero, timeout, missing repo) resolves
    to a **non-verified** Verdict — the resolver/verifier NEVER raises (fail-closed, mirrors
    `resolve_trust_root`).
- **Intra-commit refinement (N-events-per-commit):** `batch_stage_and_commit` puts N event
  files in ONE commit, and `merge-base --is-ancestor(C, C)` is true, so when a key's
  add/revoke shares the event's commit the DAG cannot order them — refine with the
  **intra-commit position** (`{HLC-ts}-{uuid}` prefix ordering, which is a total order
  within that commit): a KEY_ADD in the same commit counts as added iff its position ≤ the
  event position; a KEY_REVOKE in the same commit counts as revoked iff its position ≤ the
  event position. NOTE this HLC tiebreak is NOT the rejected inter-commit weakness: events
  in one commit were written by ONE author in ONE atomic batch, so an attacker cannot
  insert an event into someone else's already-sealed commit — intra-commit ordering is not
  attacker-exploitable the way cross-commit backdating was.

## What
- Reducer `process_key_event` folds position-based records (no epoch); `make_initial_state`
  seeds `keyring: []` (no `keyring_epoch`); `_rederive_keyring_keys` → keys with
  `revoked_at is None`; remove `keys_valid_at_epoch`.
- **Genesis / bootstrap:** keys carried on a CREATE (`_bootstrap_genesis_keyring`) get
  `added_at` = **the CREATE event's position** (`{ts}-{uuid}` of the CREATE), so their
  added-commit resolves to the CREATE commit (an ancestor of every later event). There is
  NO separate ambiguous sentinel. TOFU genesis (first `KEY_ADD` on a keyless identity)
  records `added_at` = that KEY_ADD's position.
- **Snapshot restore + v4 migration:** `process_snapshot` restores the position-based
  keyring verbatim. A pre-position (e165 v4) SNAPSHOT whose keyring records carry
  `added_epoch`/`revoked_epoch` (no `added_at`) is MIGRATED by re-seeding from the
  identity's static `keys` via `_bootstrap_genesis_keyring` (treated as genesis at the
  CREATE position) rather than trusting the stale epoch fields. Because the stack is
  UNLANDED and lands atomically, no real store holds a v4 epoch snapshot; this migration is
  a defensive floor, not a live path.
- `authorship.py`: add `resolve_event_commit` + `verify_authorship_at_commit` +
  commit-ancestry trust root; **REMOVE `verify_authorship_at_epoch`, `epoch_for_position`,
  `keys_valid_at_epoch`, `_position_prefix`'s epoch use** (the HLC-epoch API).
- `identity.py` key add/revoke seam is unchanged in shape (TOFU genesis self-signed;
  unsigned non-genesis refused; signed add/rotate/revoke); only epoch wording in docstrings
  becomes commit wording.
- Bump `SCHEMA_VERSION` v4→v5 and `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` v5→v6 (both preserve-and-ignore).
- Consumes TS1's in-toto authorship (verify the event's Statement against keys valid at the
  event's commit).

## Stack scoping (why TS2 and TS3 land together)
This story AMENDS Gerrit change 563 = commit 2efdae38f (e165), which touches ONLY
`authorship.py`, `reducer/{_processors,_state,_version,_cache}.py`, and `identity.py`. The
**consumers** of the era model — `verify_authorship.py` (`_verify_signed` → `epoch_for_position`
/ `verify_authorship_at_epoch`) and `compact.py` (`_build_authorship_ledger` → `epoch`) —
live in commit 721702e91 (3183), so they are rewired to `resolve_event_commit` /
`verify_authorship_at_commit` in **TS3 (117b, change 580)**. Because TS2 removes the epoch
API from `authorship.py` while TS3 updates its commit-7 consumers, the two are pushed
together and land atomically with the whole stack; each is internally green at its own
patchset (at commit 563 the verify-gate/compact code does not yet exist). TS2 REPLACES
e165's OWN tests (`test_identity_e165*`, which assert the epoch model) with
commit-ancestry tests.

## Scope
In: position-based key records, `resolve_event_commit`, DAG-ancestry validity
(`verify_authorship_at_commit`) with intra-commit refinement + fail-closed git handling,
TOFU/write-gate, reducer fold + snapshot + v4 migration + version bumps, removal of the
epoch API, transitioning e165's tests. Out: authorship payload format (TS1); the SNAPSHOT
ledger schema + `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict + the verify-gate/compact rewiring (TS3 /
3183, commit 7).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The resolved key record binds `{pubkey, added_at_commit, revoked_at_commit?}`
  (tickets-branch commit SHAs); a test using TWO real fixture commits asserts the resolved
  shape (add key in commit A, revoke in a later commit B; resolver yields A and B).
- [ ] Genesis key-add is accepted ONLY when self-signed (TOFU base case); an unsigned
  NON-genesis key-add is refused (test).
- [ ] Commit-ancestry validity across the revocation boundary: with key K added in commit
  C1 and revoked in a later commit C3, an event signed by K whose commit descends C1 but
  NOT C3 PASSES, and one whose commit descends C3 FAILS (test, both sides of boundary).
- [ ] Validity derives from commit ancestry, NOT event timestamps: an event written AFTER
  the revocation but carrying a BACKDATED `data`/filename timestamp still FAILS, because its
  introducing commit descends the revocation commit regardless of the timestamp (the
  killer test that commit-ancestry beats HLC backdating).
- [ ] Intra-commit refinement: with a KEY_ADD and an event in the SAME commit, a KEY_ADD at
  an earlier intra-commit position → event PASSES; a KEY_ADD at a later position → event
  FAILS; symmetrically a KEY_REVOKE at an earlier position in the same commit → event FAILS
  (test using a batched multi-event commit).
- [ ] Fail-closed git handling: when the position→commit resolution cannot run (git
  failure / no matching commit), verification returns a non-verified Verdict and never
  raises (test, e.g. an unresolvable position).
- [ ] Replay never rejects: a not-yet-valid-key / bad-signature event still folds into
  state (test); the delivered HLC-epoch surface is gone — a grep-assert confirms
  `verify_authorship_at_epoch`, `epoch_for_position`, `keys_valid_at_epoch`,
  `keyring_epoch`, `added_epoch`, `revoked_epoch` are ABSENT from `src/rebar`.
- [ ] The reducer stays pure: a grep-assert confirms `src/rebar/reducer/` imports no
  `subprocess` and runs no git (the resolver lives in `authorship.py`).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` (resolve_event_commit + verify_authorship_at_commit + commit-ancestry trust root + intra-commit refinement + fail-closed git; remove epoch API)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (position-based keyring fold; genesis added_at = CREATE position; remove keys_valid_at_epoch/epoch cursor)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_state.py` (seed keyring, no keyring_epoch)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py` (SCHEMA_VERSION v4->v5)
- `src/rebar/reducer/_cache.py` (reducer cache v5->v6)
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (docstring epoch->commit wording; seam shape unchanged)

file_impact.reason: verify_authorship_at_commit + position->commit resolver + commit-ancestry trust root; remove epoch API
position-based keyring fold; remove epoch counter/keys_valid_at_epoch
TOFU genesis vs signed non-genesis key add/revoke seam

compiled_state.title: transition: key validity -> git-commit-ancestry (tickets-branch SHAs, replaces HLC-epoch)
